description: Fix all bugs, race conditions, dead code, and quality issues found in the independent architectural review of the side panel data flow redesign. Covers: session persistence flush timing, wake detection false positives, missing cache updates, missing plugins.changed notifications, optimistic rollback correctness, reconnect race conditions, stale tab state on TOCTOU, mutable cache references, session storage merge-vs-replace, dead message types, stale comments, defensive validation, and test coverage gaps.

userStories.title: Flush session storage immediately on critical state changes, debounce only high-frequency events
userStories.description: As a platform developer, I want the session storage persistence to flush immediately after sync.full completion and disconnect cleanup so that MV3 service worker suspension during the 500ms debounce window does not lose critical state.
acceptanceCriteria: server-state-cache.ts exports a flushServerStateCacheToSession() function that writes immediately (no debounce) and cancels any pending debounced write
tab-state.ts exports a flushLastKnownStateToSession() function with the same semantics
handleSyncFull in message-router.ts calls both flush functions after populating caches (after updateServerStateCache and before the fire-and-forget sendTabSyncAll)
handleWsState disconnect path calls both flush functions after clearing caches (to persist the empty state immediately, preventing stale restoration on wake)
The 500ms debounced write-through continues for high-frequency events (individual tab.stateChanged updates from readiness polls)
Unit tests in server-state-cache.test.ts verify: flushServerStateCacheToSession writes immediately, cancels pending debounce timer
Unit tests in tab-state.test.ts verify: flushLastKnownStateToSession writes immediately, cancels pending debounce timer
userStories.notes: In `platform/browser-extension/src/server-state-cache.ts`, add `flushServerStateCacheToSession()`: cancel `persistTimer` if set, call `persistToSession()` immediately. Export it. In `platform/browser-extension/src/tab-state.ts`, add `flushLastKnownStateToSession()`: cancel the debounce timer if set, call the session write immediately. Export it. In `platform/browser-extension/src/message-router.ts` `handleSyncFull` (~line 367), after `updateServerStateCache(...)` and before the `forwardToSidePanel` call, add: `flushServerStateCacheToSession()`. Do NOT flush lastKnownState here because it's still empty at this point (sendTabSyncAll hasn't run yet). In `platform/browser-extension/src/background-message-handlers.ts` `handleWsState` disconnect path (~line 96-101), after `clearServerStateCache()` and `clearTabStateCache()` (which already clear session storage), no additional flush needed since clear already writes empty. Actually — `clearServerStateCache` already does `chrome.storage.session.remove()` and `clearTabStateCache` already clears session storage. So the flush is only needed after sync.full populates caches. Verify that `clearServerStateCache` and `clearTabStateCache` both immediately clear session storage (not debounced) — they do. The main value of this story is the post-sync.full flush.

userStories.title: Fix wake detection false positive when caches are empty between connect and sync.full
userStories.description: As a platform developer, I want the wake detection heuristic in bg:getFullState to distinguish between 'service worker woke from suspension' and 'WebSocket just connected but sync.full has not arrived yet' so that stale session data is not restored during the connect-to-sync.full gap.
acceptanceCriteria: A module-level boolean flag (e.g., cachesInitialized) is set to true in handleSyncFull after populating caches, and reset to false in handleWsState disconnect path
The flag persists to chrome.storage.session alongside the caches so it survives suspension
bg:getFullState wake detection checks cachesInitialized: if false (caches were never populated this session), do NOT restore from session storage — return empty state
bg:getFullState wake detection: if cachesInitialized is true AND in-memory caches are empty AND wsConnected is true, then restore from session storage (real wake scenario)

Unit tests in background-message-handlers.test.ts verify: (1) fresh connect with empty caches returns empty plugins (no session restore), (2) after sync.full + suspension + wake, session data is restored
userStories.notes: The core issue: in `platform/browser-extension/src/background-message-handlers.ts` line 135, the heuristic `wsConnected && tabStates.size === 0 && serverCache.plugins.length === 0` triggers both on real wake AND on the gap between WebSocket connect and sync.full arrival. Add a `cachesInitialized` flag. Where to set it: in `platform/browser-extension/src/message-router.ts` `handleSyncFull` after `updateServerStateCache` call (~line 367). Export a setter `setCachesInitialized(value: boolean)` from a suitable module (could be server-state-cache.ts since it manages server state). Reset it to false in `handleWsState` disconnect path in background-message-handlers.ts. For session persistence: include `cachesInitialized` in the session storage write for server-state-cache (or as a separate session key). In `loadServerStateCacheFromSession`, also restore the flag. In bg:getFullState: change the wake detection to `if (wsConnected && cachesInitialized && tabStates.size === 0 && serverCache.plugins.length === 0)`. When `cachesInitialized` is false (connect-to-sync.full gap), don't try to restore — just return empty/default state.

userStories.title: Update server state cache in handlePluginUninstall
userStories.description: As a user, I want the side panel to immediately remove an uninstalled plugin's card without waiting for the next plugins.changed from the server.
acceptanceCriteria: handlePluginUninstall in message-router.ts calls updateServerStateCache() to remove the plugin from the cached plugins array after removePlugin() and clearPluginTabState()
handlePluginUninstall forwards plugins.changed to the side panel after updating the cache
Unit test in message-router.test.ts verifies: after handlePluginUninstall, the server state cache no longer contains the uninstalled plugin and plugins.changed is forwarded to the side panel
userStories.notes: In `platform/browser-extension/src/message-router.ts`, `handlePluginUninstall` (line 436). After line 471 (`clearPluginTabState(pluginName)`), before sending the success response, add: (1) `const existingCache = getServerStateCache(); updateServerStateCache({ plugins: existingCache.plugins.filter(p => p.name !== pluginName) });` (2) `forwardToSidePanel({ type: 'sp:serverMessage', data: { jsonrpc: '2.0', method: 'plugins.changed' } });`. Import `getServerStateCache` and `updateServerStateCache` from `./server-state-cache.js` (already imported in this file for handleSyncFull and handlePluginUpdate). The server will also send its own `plugins.changed` after receiving the success response, which will be handled by handleServerMessage and update the cache again — this is fine (idempotent).

userStories.title: Add plugins.changed notification to config.setToolEnabled and config.setAllToolsEnabled on the MCP server
userStories.description: As a user, I want the side panel to reflect tool enabled/disabled changes made via the CLI or config file, not just changes made from the side panel itself.
acceptanceCriteria: handleConfigSetToolEnabled in extension-handlers.ts sends plugins.changed with buildConfigStatePayload(state) after updating toolConfig
handleConfigSetAllToolsEnabled in extension-handlers.ts sends plugins.changed with buildConfigStatePayload(state) after updating toolConfig
Both send plugins.changed BEFORE the success response (same pattern as handleConfigSetBrowserToolEnabled)
Existing extension-handlers.test.ts tests for config.setToolEnabled and config.setAllToolsEnabled are updated to verify plugins.changed is sent with full payload
userStories.notes: In `platform/mcp-server/src/extension-handlers.ts`: (1) `handleConfigSetToolEnabled` (line 324): after `callbacks.onToolConfigPersist()` (line 359) and before the success response (line 361), add: `sendToExtension(state, { jsonrpc: '2.0', method: 'plugins.changed', params: { ...buildConfigStatePayload(state) } });`. (2) `handleConfigSetAllToolsEnabled` (line 368): after `callbacks.onToolConfigPersist()` (line 399) and before the success response (line 401), add the same line. This matches the pattern already used by `handleConfigSetBrowserToolEnabled` (line 437) and `handleConfigSetAllBrowserToolsEnabled` (line 471). In `platform/mcp-server/src/extension-handlers.test.ts`, find the tests for config.setToolEnabled (line ~1320) and config.setAllToolsEnabled (line ~1701). Add assertions that the `send` callback (which captures messages sent to the extension) includes a plugins.changed message with params containing plugins, failedPlugins, browserTools, serverVersion.

userStories.title: Fix optimistic rollback in tool toggle handlers to capture original state
userStories.description: As a platform developer, I want tool toggle rollback to use the captured original state instead of toggling !enabled, preventing incorrect state when concurrent toggles of the same tool interleave.
acceptanceCriteria: handleBgSetToolEnabled captures the original plugins array before mutation (same pattern as handleBgSetAllToolsEnabled) and reverts to it on failure
handleBgSetBrowserToolEnabled captures the original browserTools array before mutation and reverts to it on failure
Unit tests in background-message-handlers.test.ts verify: the revert restores the exact pre-mutation state, not a toggled version
userStories.notes: In `platform/browser-extension/src/background-message-handlers.ts`: (1) `handleBgSetToolEnabled` (line 294): Add `const originalPlugins = cache.plugins;` before `const updatedPlugins = ...` (line 301). In the catch block (line 314-326), change the revert to `updateServerStateCache({ plugins: originalPlugins })` instead of the current per-tool `!enabled` toggle logic. (2) `handleBgSetBrowserToolEnabled` (line 358): Add `const originalBrowserTools = cache.browserTools;` before `const updatedBrowserTools = ...` (line 364). In the catch block (line 371-378), change to `updateServerStateCache({ browserTools: originalBrowserTools })`. Note: `handleBgSetAllToolsEnabled` (line 330) and `handleBgSetAllBrowserToolsEnabled` (line 383) already correctly capture `originalPlugins`/`originalBrowserTools` — no change needed for those.

userStories.title: Fix loadLastKnownStateFromSession to clear Map before populating
userStories.description: As a platform developer, I want loadLastKnownStateFromSession to replace the Map contents instead of merging, preventing stale entries from mixing with restored session data.
acceptanceCriteria: loadLastKnownStateFromSession in tab-state.ts clears the lastKnownState Map before populating from session storage
If session storage has no data or is corrupted, the Map remains empty (not cleared then left empty — same as current behavior but explicit)
Existing tab-state.test.ts tests for session storage load are updated to verify: pre-existing Map entries are cleared before restoration
userStories.notes: In `platform/browser-extension/src/tab-state.ts`, `loadLastKnownStateFromSession` (line 281). Add `lastKnownState.clear()` at the start of the function body, before the try block. This ensures that even if session storage read fails or returns empty data, the Map starts fresh. Currently the function adds entries to the existing Map via `lastKnownState.set(key, value)` without clearing first. If the Map was partially populated (e.g., a sync.full raced with session restoration), old entries and new entries would mix. In `platform/browser-extension/src/tab-state.test.ts`, add a test: pre-populate lastKnownState with an entry, call loadLastKnownStateFromSession with different data in session storage, verify the old entry is gone and only the session data entries remain.

userStories.title: Return deep copy from getServerStateCache to prevent accidental mutation
userStories.description: As a platform developer, I want getServerStateCache to return a deep copy so that callers cannot silently corrupt the in-memory cache by mutating nested arrays.
acceptanceCriteria: getServerStateCache in server-state-cache.ts returns a structuredClone of the cache instead of the live object reference
Unit tests in server-state-cache.test.ts verify: mutating the returned object does not affect the internal cache state
userStories.notes: In `platform/browser-extension/src/server-state-cache.ts`, line 52: change `const getServerStateCache = (): Readonly<ServerStateCache> => cache;` to `const getServerStateCache = (): ServerStateCache => structuredClone(cache);`. Remove the `Readonly<>` wrapper since deep copy already protects against mutation. In `platform/browser-extension/src/server-state-cache.test.ts`, add a test: call getServerStateCache, mutate the returned plugins array (e.g., push a new element), call getServerStateCache again, verify the new element is NOT present.

userStories.title: Remove dead code: bg:getConnectionState handler, sp:confirmationRequest type, tab.stateChanged from SIDE_PANEL_METHODS
userStories.description: As a platform developer, I want dead message types and unreachable SIDE_PANEL_METHODS entries removed so the codebase accurately reflects the actual data flow.
acceptanceCriteria: BgGetConnectionStateMessage type removed from extension-messages.ts and InternalMessage union
handleBgGetConnectionState handler removed from background-message-handlers.ts and backgroundHandlers map

SpConfirmationRequestMessage type removed from extension-messages.ts and InternalMessage union (confirmation requests reach the side panel via sp:serverMessage with method 'confirmation.request', not via this dead type)

'tab.stateChanged' removed from SIDE_PANEL_METHODS in message-router.ts (tab state changes are sent to the side panel directly via sendTabStateNotification/forwardToSidePanel, not through handleServerMessage/SIDE_PANEL_METHODS)
known-methods.test.ts passes (verify no test depends on the removed entries)
userStories.notes: THREE dead code removals: (1) `bg:getConnectionState`: In `extension-messages.ts` remove the `BgGetConnectionStateMessage` interface (~line 51-53) and remove it from the `InternalMessage` union. In `background-message-handlers.ts` remove `handleBgGetConnectionState` function (~line 112-118) and remove `['bg:getConnectionState', handleBgGetConnectionState]` from the backgroundHandlers map. Remove 'bg:getConnectionState' from EXTENSION_ONLY_TYPES. Remove the case from offscreen/index.ts switch. (2) `sp:confirmationRequest`: In `extension-messages.ts` remove the `SpConfirmationRequestMessage` interface (~line 110-120) and remove from InternalMessage union. Remove from offscreen/index.ts switch (line 502). (3) `tab.stateChanged` in SIDE_PANEL_METHODS: In `message-router.ts` line 106, remove `'tab.stateChanged'` from the set. The server NEVER sends tab.stateChanged to the extension — the extension sends it TO the server and TO the side panel directly via `sendTabStateNotification` in messaging.ts. The `SIDE_PANEL_METHODS` check only applies to messages routed through `handleServerMessage` (server→extension), so this entry is unreachable.

userStories.title: Fix stale comment in bridge.ts and add defensive validation in getAggregateState
userStories.description: As a platform developer, I want comments to accurately describe current behavior and runtime types to be validated defensively.
acceptanceCriteria: bridge.ts BrowserToolState type comment updated to reference bg:getFullState instead of config.getState
getAggregateState in tab-state.ts validates that parsed.state is one of 'closed', 'unavailable', 'ready' before returning, defaulting to 'closed' for unexpected values
consumeServerResponse in server-request.ts checks that data.error is truthy (not just 'in' check) before treating as error, preventing false rejection for { error: undefined }
handleWsMessage in background-message-handlers.ts wraps handleServerMessage in try-catch to ensure sendResponse is always called
Unit test for getAggregateState with corrupted state field verifies fallback to 'closed'
Unit test for consumeServerResponse with { id: 1, error: undefined, result: 'ok' } verifies it resolves (not rejects)
userStories.notes: Four small fixes: (1) `platform/browser-extension/src/side-panel/bridge.ts` line 24: change comment from 'matches config.getState response' to 'matches bg:getFullState response'. (2) `platform/browser-extension/src/tab-state.ts` `getAggregateState` (line 298-305): after `JSON.parse`, validate: `const validStates = new Set(['closed', 'unavailable', 'ready']); return validStates.has(parsed.state) ? parsed.state : 'closed';`. (3) `platform/browser-extension/src/server-request.ts` `consumeServerResponse` (line 73): change `if ('error' in data)` to `if (data.error != null)` — this correctly handles both `{ error: { message: '...' } }` (real error) and `{ error: undefined }` (should resolve, not reject). (4) `platform/browser-extension/src/background-message-handlers.ts` `handleWsMessage` (line 107-110): wrap `handleServerMessage(message.data)` in try-catch, log the error, and ensure `sendResponse({ ok: true })` is called in both success and error paths.

userStories.title: Add missing unit test coverage for security-critical paths and untested functions
userStories.description: As a platform developer, I want comprehensive unit test coverage for the EXTENSION_ONLY_TYPES security guard, sendConfirmationResponse, handleConfigSetAllBrowserToolsEnabled, and other untested paths.
acceptanceCriteria: background-message-handlers.test.ts includes tests for EXTENSION_ONLY_TYPES: content script messages with bg:* types are rejected, extension context messages are accepted
bridge.test.ts includes tests for sendConfirmationResponse: verifies correct message type and data sent, handles chrome.runtime.sendMessage rejection gracefully
extension-handlers.test.ts includes tests for handleConfigSetAllBrowserToolsEnabled: valid toggle, invalid params, plugins.changed sent with full payload
server-request.test.ts includes test for consumeServerResponse with { id, error: undefined, result: 'data' } — verifies resolution not rejection
tab-state.test.ts includes test for getAggregateState with corrupted state values — verifies fallback to 'closed'
userStories.notes: Multiple test files need additions. (1) `platform/browser-extension/src/background-message-handlers.test.ts`: add a describe block for 'EXTENSION_ONLY_TYPES security'. Test that sending a message with type 'bg:setToolEnabled' from a MessageSender with a `tab` property (content script) is rejected (handler not called, or returns error). Test that the same message from a sender without `tab` (extension popup/page) is accepted. Look at how the existing listener registration uses `sender` in the actual code — `background-message-handlers.ts` line ~510-530 area. (2) `platform/browser-extension/src/side-panel/bridge.test.ts`: add test for sendConfirmationResponse — mock chrome.runtime.sendMessage, verify it's called with `{ type: 'sp:confirmationResponse', data: { id, decision, scope } }`. (3) `platform/mcp-server/src/extension-handlers.test.ts`: add tests for handleConfigSetAllBrowserToolsEnabled — follow the pattern of existing handleConfigSetBrowserToolEnabled tests. Verify valid toggle sends plugins.changed with full payload, invalid params return -32602 error. (4) Tests for consumeServerResponse and getAggregateState as described in US-009 acceptance criteria.

userStories.title: Fix E2E test quality: scope amber dot selector, verify disconnect state in reconnect test, remove double-fire in config test
userStories.description: As a platform developer, I want E2E tests to test exactly what they claim by using correctly scoped selectors and verifying intermediate states.
acceptanceCriteria: side-panel-realtime-state.e2e.ts: amber dot assertion is scoped to the e2e plugin card (e2ePluginCard.locator('.bg-primary.rounded-full')) instead of sidePanelPage.locator('.bg-primary.rounded-full').first()
side-panel-realtime-state.e2e.ts: reconnect test verifies the plugin card disappears or disconnected state appears during the disconnect window before asserting recovery

side-panel-realtime-state.e2e.ts: config change test removes the redundant POST /reload call (relies only on the config file watcher), OR removes the config file write (relies only on POST /reload) — pick one mechanism and test it cleanly
userStories.notes: In `e2e/side-panel-realtime-state.e2e.ts`: (1) Find the amber dot assertion (~line 168). Currently: `sidePanelPage.locator('.bg-primary.rounded-full').first()`. Change to: `e2ePluginCard.locator('.bg-primary.rounded-full')` where e2ePluginCard is the scoped locator for the E2E Test plugin card (same pattern used for green dot assertions). (2) Find the reconnect/hot-reload test. It should assert that the plugin card disappears OR the 'Cannot Reach MCP Server' text appears during the disconnect window, THEN assert recovery. Without this intermediate assertion, the test passes even if no disconnect happened. (3) Find the config change test (~line 94-96). It both writes config AND calls POST /reload — two competing update mechanisms. Remove the POST /reload call and rely on the file watcher alone (this is what we're testing). If the file watcher path is too slow/unreliable in the test environment, reverse: keep POST /reload and remove the config file write. Read `e2e/CLAUDE.md` before making changes.
